summary: Implemented the minimal deterministic M5 playable simulation loop by extending the accepted M4 determinism replay into an M3 postwar governance success path, a save/load round-trip, a duplicate-settlement failure path, and Node/worker-compatible hash canary coverage. The loop stays in sim-core/protocol/save-format/test surfaces and does not touch UI/client/content packages.

tests_run.result: Initial pre-edit run was clean; final run contains only the expected M5 implementation, state, and handoff files.
23 test files passed; 170 tests passed. Includes tests/sim-core-m5-playable-loop.test.ts and updated save/load contract coverage.
pnpm reported: No projects matched the filters in D:\WebProjects\MonsoonSovereigns. No packages/sim-ai package exists in this repo.
ERR_PNPM_RECURSIVE_RUN_NO_SCRIPT: selected package has no typecheck script.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched for hello, command/query, save/load, M4 replay, and M5 playable loop. M5 final hash bf042e42; loaded hash bf042e42; postwar arrangements 1.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism canary passed; root vitest 34 files/242 tests passed; web test 1 file/4 tests passed.

decisions: Used existing m4.determinism-replay-001 and accepted M4 command sequence as the M5 start-to-postwar substrate.
Used the existing M3 tribute-only postwar governance path for the M5 success branch to avoid expanding content fixtures with new character/policy dependencies.
Kept player and AI parity through versioned GameCommandV1 only; the AI actor remains the existing M4 muster response command and does not read hidden state.
Added M4 runtime save-format support at explicit top-level M4 slice boundaries with decode-time object validation and sim-core validateWorldStateV0 semantic validation for nested M4 fields.
Did not create packages/sim-ai because this repository currently has no @monsoon/sim-ai package and accepted M4 AI surfaces live in sim-core/protocol.
risks: R4 task with R3 save/protocol impact: save-format now persists M4 runtime state. Systems architecture review should confirm whether the top-level explicit M4 DTO plus sim-core semantic validation is sufficient or whether a full nested M4 save DTO should be split into a follow-up.
Worker-compatible M5 coverage uses the same sim-core runner source through a local adapter in tests and sim-runner because apps/web is forbidden for this task. No apps/web worker export was changed.
No UI/client/manual battle/full diplomacy/naval/telemetry/server/multiplayer/content-source work was included.
requested_action: Review the deterministic M5 loop, M4 save-format schema evolution, event-tail parsing, and Node/worker-compatible hash coverage. If accepted, route onward to qa_reviewer.
